description: America's Finest City — a sun-soaked Pacific beach city on the US-Mexico border

description: The Cradle of the Renaissance — a compact city of unparalleled art, the Uffizi and the Duomo

name: Brisbane, Australia
description: Sunny Queensland capital on the Brisbane River, host city of the 2032 Olympic Games

name: Perth, Australia

name: Auckland, New Zealand
description: New Zealand's largest city, straddling two harbours on a slender volcanic isthmus

description: Festival city of South Australia, famous for wine regions, world-class museums and a planned grid

name: Wellington, New Zealand
description: Compact, wind-blown capital of New Zealand perched on a stunning harbour at the Cook Strait

description: Jewel of the French Riviera, a pastel city of beaches, belle époque promenades and Matisse

description: The Pink City — France's aerospace capital on the Garonne, home of Airbus

description: Rhineland city dominated by its twin-towered Gothic cathedral, Germany's carnival capital

name: Turin, Italy

name: Bologna, Italy

description: Best-preserved medieval old town in Northern Europe, a Baltic gem of turrets and spires

name: Riga, Latvia
description: Art Nouveau capital of Latvia — the largest city in the Baltic states

name: Birmingham, UK
description: England's second city — a multicultural powerhouse that gave the world the industrial revolution

description: Major oasis city deep in the Algerian Sahara, gateway to the Hoggar Mountains and Tuareg territory

description: Abandoned diamond-mining ghost town slowly swallowed by Namib Desert sand dunes since the 1950s

description: World's second-largest geyser field, discovered in 1941 in a remote volcanic valley in Russia's Far East

description: Ancient Saharan trading city and UNESCO World Heritage site, heart of the Tuareg homeland in northern Niger

name: Zion National Park, USA
description: A dramatic red-rock canyon carved by the Virgin River, known for its towering sandstone cliffs, narrow slot canyons, and breathtaking desert vistas in southwestern Utah.
